title: Nesting elements
description: Parents, children, and why closing order matters.

questions.prompt: What visible text does this render?
questions.explanation: Nested inline elements are all rendered inline. Text: 'Hi there friend!'

options: Ignored by the browser but helpful for humans
questions.explanation: The parser ignores extra whitespace; use indentation for readability.

Historically block inside inline was invalid. In HTML5, can wrap block content — but
inside

is not allowed.

questions.prompt: Nested lists — the correct pattern is:
questions.explanation: A nested list must live INSIDE an <li>.

The parser auto-closes an open

when a new one starts.
<div> is block by default; <span>/<em>/<a> are inline.
